Keys:  R record · Esc cancel · P play the highlighted action · Shift+P whole folder

Save a copy — a numbered file in your quick-copy folder (Ctrl+E)

Manoni can speak any language. Here's how:
1. Generate a template file — every text in the app, with your
    current language already filled in where it has one.
2. Open it in any text editor and translate the lines still in English.
3. Import the finished file — your language appears in the menu.

Updating a pack for a newer Manoni works the same way: generate,
translate what is new, import.
Save a .mnl file with every text to translate
Load a finished .mnl translation and install it
Save an installed language to a .mnl file to share
Tilt to level the horizon (the crop trims the corners)
Add your size to the list
Reset the selection to the whole image
Name it and set width : height (pixels or a ratio, e.g. 4:5).
The crop area is too small
The whole image is selected — nothing changes
Cropped → {w}×{h}px  ·  Save to write it to a file
Discard every edit and reload the original photo
Hold to see the original — press for before, release for after
Costly: every later edit pays for it — switch it on last
Adds cost to later edits — better switched on late
Saves the current slider values as a filter
Undoes whatever filter(s) you've tried, back to before the first one
Costly: every later edit pays for it — apply this look last
Delete the group “{name}” and its {n} filter(s)?
Drag the band to set the focus; an edge changes its width, the end dot rotates it. Sharp in the band, blurred outside.
Drag the circle to set the focus; the edge resizes it. Sharp inside, blurred outside.
On: the source keeps the same offset from the brush across every stroke. Off: each new stroke re-anchors to the picked source point.
Mirror the source left-right about its point — handy for symmetric retouching (e.g. copying from the other eye).
Remove every retouch on this photo
Alt+click — pick a source; then paint an exact copy. The wheel or [ ] changes the brush size.
Click or drag over a blemish — I erase it with nearby clean background, or Alt+click to pick your own source. The wheel or [ ] changes the brush size.
Source picked — now paint the copy
Can't undo — a different image is open
Manoni is a fast photo browser, culler and editor. The whole workflow, in five steps:
Pick a folder of photos — thumbnails fill the sidebar.
The arrow keys step through the photos; click a thumbnail to jump.
Enter keeps and Backspace rejects — each photo moves to its folder.
Open the edit panel to adjust light, colour, crop and more.
Export the result as JPEG, PNG or WEBP.
Each tab on the left explains one part in more detail.

The app comes in English and Polish. Every other language is a pack: download one from the Discussions board and double-click it, or build your own — Settings → General → Add your language writes a template you translate and import. No code needed, and anything left untranslated simply stays English.
You browse the photos and sort each into a keep folder and a discard folder — nothing is deleted, only moved.
Moves the current photo to the keep folder.
Moves the current photo to the discard folder.
Choose the keep and reject folders in Settings first — until then these buttons do nothing.
At the end of a folder Manoni can loop back to the first photo or open the next folder — set this in Settings → Culling.
These work whether the edit panel is open or closed; if the photo has unsaved edits, Manoni saves a copy or asks first (Settings → Culling → Auto-save).
Save a copy — a numbered file in the quick-copy folder.
Save as… — write a new copy.
Replay the highlighted action on the open photo.
Replay it over the whole folder.
Toggle the hand in the top bar, then drag to move the photo.
Drag the split line, or hold it to peek at the original.
The chevron on the icon rail (right of the photo) opens and closes the edit panel. The tools:
Exposure, contrast, white balance, highlights and shadows.
Per-hue saturation, plus dedicated gold and skin tuning.
Clarity, sharpen, denoise, dehaze, grain and vignette.
Trim, straighten a tilted horizon (±45°), rotate 90° and flip horizontally or vertically; ratio, social and your own saved presets.
Change the pixel dimensions — one photo, or a whole folder (optionally its subfolders), with a progress bar you can cancel.
Correct keystoning and straighten converging lines.
Paint over blemishes; Alt+click sets a clone source.
Blur the surroundings and keep the subject sharp.
Add live text overlays — many per photo, snap to a corner.
Ctrl+Z steps back through your edits; the before/after button compares the result with the original.
A filter is a saved slider preset — click it to apply the whole look at once.
The strip of previews under the photo shows each filter on the current photo; click one to apply it.
The moment you save a photo, its edit is pinned as a “Last” slot in the strip and the filter list — click it to apply the same look to the next photo, no saving needed.
“Last” lasts only for this session; its … menu can promote it into a permanent named filter, or clear it.
Dial in an edit you like, then save it as a filter to reuse on any photo.
Rename, reorder or delete your filters from the Filters panel.
A filter group's … menu exports the whole group to a small .mnf file — send it to a friend, and they load it from the Import button pinned atop the Filters panel.
Sharing works per group, not per single filter: export a group, import the file someone sends you.
An action is a recorded macro of edits you can replay on other photos.
Arm the recorder, make your edits, then stop — the steps are saved as an action.
Play an action on the open photo, or batch it over a whole folder.
Heal strokes are not recorded; everything else — light, colour, crop, text, filters — is.
P uses the last action you recorded or played — shown in the panel in the accent colour. These keys pause while you type a name.

Ctrl+S (or the Save button under the edit panel, and the Save button in the top bar) writes your edits straight back onto the open file, replacing it. There is no backup, so the first time it asks you to confirm.

Ctrl+E (or the copy button in the top bar) saves the same edits as a NEW, numbered file in your quick-copy folder — no dialog, and neither the original nor an earlier copy is replaced. Pick the folder in Settings → Export.
Ctrl+Shift+S (or Save as… in the top bar) opens a dialog to write a NEW file — pick the format (JPEG / PNG / WEBP), quality and folder. The original is left untouched.

Turn on Settings → Culling → Auto-save, and each time you step away from or cull an edited photo Manoni silently drops a copy in the export folder — no clicks. With it off, it asks (Save a copy / Discard) before you leave.
A ● in front of the file name — in the bottom bar and the window title — means the photo has edits not yet saved anywhere. It disappears the moment you save.
Save as… and the culling copy default to a subfolder next to each photo, or to one fixed folder — set it in Settings → Export.

The info button on the bottom bar — the strip showing which file you're editing right now — opens the photo's metadata, and its red Delete metadata button wipes the colour profile and all EXIF (including GPS) from the file, keeping the pixels exactly.
The same camera info, date, GPS and colour profile can also be kept or removed when you save a copy.
The overwrite confirmation and every export default live in Settings → Export.
Add your own transparent PNG logo
Recolour the whole logo to one flat colour
Remove every logo from the photo
No saved logos yet — use Choose PNG…

Remove “{name}” from your saved logos?
The PNG file will be deleted from disk.
This photo has no embedded metadata (no colour profile or EXIF).

Permanently remove the colour profile and all EXIF (including GPS location) from “{name}”?

The pixels are kept exactly; this can't be undone.
no folder set yet — choose it in  ⚙ Settings
Wrapped around to the last photo
Wrapped around to the first photo
Nothing to restore — no edits yet
The original is back — every edit was cleared
Discard every edit on {fname} and reload the original from disk?
Write your edits straight onto {fname}, replacing it — no backup, can't be undone. Or save a copy instead.
Where should the arrow keys go next?
Set the sorting folders first  ·  ⚙ Settings
Move this photo to the Reject folder?
Set where kept and rejected photos go. Until both are set, the buttons don't work.
Folders incomplete — culling doesn't work yet
Can't undo the edit — a different folder is open
Can't undo the edit — the file no longer exists
Straighten converging verticals or horizontals (buildings shot from below / to the side).
Tilt the top/bottom — fix verticals that lean in or out
Tilt the left/right — fix horizontals that lean in or out
Perspective applied  ·  Save to write it to a file
The original stays untouched — Save writes the resized copy.
Resize every photo in a folder with the size above — pick the folder, subfolders and where the copies go. Originals are untouched.
Soft = smoother · Sharp adds web output-sharpening.
Reset the size to the current photo
Resized → {w}×{h}px  ·  Save to write it to a file
A new subfolder in each folder
Can't overwrite this file type — use Save as…
Choose the folder your copies go to
Keep metadata (camera info, GPS, colour profile)
Convert colours to sRGB (best for web)
Switching relaunches Manoni and restores your place.
Generate a template, translate it, then import it.
Jump back to the last photo
A quick confirm before a photo moves to the Reject folder.
Warn about unsaved edits when leaving a photo
Switch between the dark and light interface.
The row of filter previews under the photo.
The live tonal graph at the top of the edit panel.
The top and left rulers over the photo (Ctrl+R).
Skip the heavy filters (clarity, sharpen, denoise, dehaze, focus, grain) while a slider is dragged; full quality returns the moment you let go.

Do the heavy edit work on a background thread so the window never freezes while a costly effect renders; the photo catches up a moment behind the slider. Turn off to render on the main thread (the older behaviour).
A small coloured dot beside the sliders and filters that make every later edit slower — red for the heaviest, amber for the rest. Switch those on last.
Used for JPEG and WEBP (PNG is always lossless).
Camera info, date, GPS and the colour profile.
Best for the web — keeps colours consistent across browsers.
These are the defaults the Save dialog opens with.
Ctrl+E and the copy button write your edits here as a new, numbered file — the original is never touched, and an earlier copy is never replaced. Left unset, the first copy asks for the folder.
Ctrl+S and the Save button write your edits back over the open file. Keep this on to confirm first — there is no backup of the original.
Created next to each photo (e.g. folder/_edited).
The keep / reject buttons (and the Enter / Backspace keys) move the current photo into these folders. Ctrl+Z undoes the last move.
Save an edited copy when you move on
As you step away from or cull an edited photo, silently write a copy to the export folder — no prompt. Off shows the usual Save / Discard prompt instead.
At the end of the folder
When you pass the last photo
← / → past the edge of the folder.
“Ask” pops a small chooser each time you reach the edge. “First photo” loops back; “Next folder” opens the next folder that has photos.
a fast, simple photo browser, culler and editor
Questions, bugs or ideas — write to me directly.
Manoni is a free tool I build in my spare time. I'm also available for paid, custom work:
Programs and games — small to medium complexity
